fields.name: Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code you want to assign to this tax jurisdiction. You can enter up to 10 characters, both numbers and letters. It is a good idea to enter a code that is easy to remember.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the tax jurisdiction. For example, if the tax jurisdiction code is ATLANTA GA, enter the description as Atlanta, Georgia.

fields.name: Tax Account (Sales)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general ledger account you want to use for posting calculated tax on sales transactions.

fields.name: Tax Account (Purchases)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general ledger account you want to use for posting calculated tax on purchase transactions.

fields.name: Report-to Jurisdiction
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax jurisdiction you want to associate with the jurisdiction you are setting up. For example, if you are setting up a jurisdiction for Atlanta, Georgia, the report-to jurisdiction is Georgia because Georgia is the tax authority to which you report Atlanta sales tax.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Tax Group Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies a tax group code to set up a filter that the program will use to select the transactions that will be included in the Sales Tax Collected report.

fields.name: Unreal. Tax Acc. (Sales)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general ledger account you want to use for posting calculated unrealized tax on sales transactions.

fields.name: Unreal. Tax Acc. (Purchases)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general ledger account you want to use for posting calculated unrealized tax on purchase transactions.

fields.name: Reverse Charge (Purchases)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general ledger account you want to use for posting calculated reverse-charge tax on purchase transactions.

fields.name: Unreal. Rev. Charge (Purch.)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the general ledger account you want to use for posting calculated unrealized reverse-charge tax on purchase transactions.

fields.name: Unrealized VAT Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to handle unrealized tax, which is tax that is calculated but not due until the invoice is paid.

fields.name: Calculate Tax on Tax
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to calculate the sales tax amount with the tax on tax principle.

fields.name: Adjust for Payment Discount
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to recalculate tax amounts when you post payments that trigger payment discounts.
